calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar May-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Craig Kisylia, Esq. 415-981-0550 Lubin Olson & Niewiadomski for Petitioner.
Alfred Bay, Esq. 510-812-9184 for Defendant Edgar Calderon who is also present. (In-person)

The Clerk informs both sides the Court did not receive any notice to contest this matter. Mr. Kisylia states he did not receive any notice to contest the Court's TR. Mr. Bay states he did not send any notice to contest the Court's TR.

At the instruction of the Court, the clerk informs parties the matter will not be heard. The Court's tentative ruling will be the final ruling of the Court.

RULING - The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER AXEL8 CONSTRUCTION, LLC's Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award.

Good cause appearing, the Petition to Confirm the Arbitrator's Award is granted and the 9/24/2025 Arbitration Award is confirmed in its entirety. (Code of Civil Procedure section 1286.) Judgment shall be entered consistent with the 9/24/2025 Award. (Code of Civil Procedure section 1287.4.)

Petitoner to submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling, a proposed judgment in compliance with CRC 3.1590(j)and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Apr-16-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Petitioner Axel8 Construction, LLC's Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award is OFF CALENDAR,

Defendants have not demonstrated timely filing or service. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address and place of mailing are within State of adds 5 calendar days to the period of notice]; see Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(e) [service by fax only be agreement and adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Defendants filed the motion on 4/10/2026, which is not 16 court days before 4/16/2026. This is the end of the inquiry.

Petitioner may be relying on Code of Civil Procedure section 1290.2. That section reads: "A petition under this title shall be heard in a summary way in the manner and upon the notice provided by law for the making and hearing of motions, except that not less than 10 days' notice of the date set for the hearing on the petition shall be given." "[T]he notice provided by law for the making and hearing of motions" is set out in section 1005, which is discussed above and requires 16 days' notice. The last clause is a reference to the rules for shortening time but prohibits shortening that gives respondents less than ten days' notice. Even if ten days' notice were all that was required, Petitioner's notice still falls short by several days.

Additionally, the court has no courtesy copies of the moving papers. (See LRSF, rule 2.7(B).) All futures filings must be accompanied by timely submission of courtesy copies to the department.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Petitioner shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order repeating the above verbatim.

No proposed order received. Plaintiff failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
